rationale: Load-bearing assertion about friction as the primary cause of activation failure. Satisfies decontextualization (mechanism stated, conditions clear). Specific, standalone-meaningful claim about product-user interaction.
Conditional claim about e-commerce economics at scale. Decontextualized, conditions specified, mechanism provided. Falsifiable structure about structural CAC shift.

Conditional claim about founder-led recruiting when founder has domain expertise. Conditions specified (domain expertise required), mechanism clear (rapid quality judgment). Load-bearing for hiring decisions.
Conditional assertion about CEO-led outreach effectiveness. Conditions present (CEO as sender, senior roles), mechanism stated (credibility signal). Falsifiable claim about response rates.

Mechanism claim about indirect referral framing reducing social pressure in executive recruiting. Specific technique with stated mechanism (loss-aversion incentive). Load-bearing for recruitment strategy.

Conditional mechanism about sequential hiring priority. Conditions specified (multiple unfilled roles, founder managing), mechanism clear (prevents onboarding degradation). Falsifiable hiring principle.
Conditional claim about CEO involvement levels by seniority. Conditions present (capacity required, scale consideration), boundary stated (directors excluded). Load-bearing for hiring structure.

Mechanism claim about pattern-matching skill development through hiring cycles. Specific, decontextualized: describes how template-building must repeat per role/level. Falsifiable principle about skill accumulation.

Assertion about leveraging domain experts for technical hiring vetting. Conditions present (paid relationships exist, technical role), no stated mechanism but functional logic is clear. Recruitment practice.
Mechanism claim about cringe as defensive status signal. Stated causal chain (status shift triggers discomfort). Load-bearing reframe of social signal interpretation.
Assertion distinguishing three social-signal terms. Decontextualized definitions with stated referents. Clarifies cringe vs. shame vs. guilt as distinct concepts. Falsifiable definitional claim.

Assertion about early-stage documentation's role in comeback narrative. Conditions present (belief in eventual success), mechanism stated (narrative leverage). Load-bearing for documentation strategy.
Assertion about judging others reflecting self-judgment. No explicit mechanism stated, but claim is standalone-meaningful about psychological projection. Falsifiable psychological principle.
Mechanism claim about willingness to remain incompetent as prerequisite for skill development. Stated causal chain (behavioral feedback loops). Load-bearing for learning theory.
Assertion about comparing different chapters being a category error. Decontextualized, falsifiable claim about comparison bias in discouragement. Load-bearing for growth psychology.
Assertion that early-stage output is universally low quality. Specific claim supported by personal examples (Hormozi's evidence). Load-bearing reframe of quality expectations.
Mechanism claim about skilled performers being poor explainers. Stated causal logic (automatic skill + reinforced verbal response). Falsifiable claim about teaching vs. performing.
Mechanism claim about observable-only focus accelerating learning. Causal statement (feedback loops vs. narratives). Load-bearing for learning methodology.
Assertion that skills are chains of adapted behaviors. Decontextualized definition of skill structure. Load-bearing framework for skill decomposition.
Mechanism claim about observing actions vs. self-reports. Stated causal logic (automatic behavior vs. verbal report). Falsifiable principle for modeling high performers.
Mechanism about observation density improving per iteration. Causal statement (skill training enables finer pattern recognition). Load-bearing for iterative learning.
Mechanism about luck-attribution terminating search. Causal claim (attribution impacts search behavior). Load-bearing reframe for performance causation.
Quantified claim with anchors (99/100 cases vs. 1/100 cases). Fits L2b: gradient axis about superior performers. Self-reported observation bounds it as directional.
Assertion about failure knowledge vs. unexplained success. Attribution to Professor Bergman; falsifiable claim about causality discoverability. Load-bearing for learning feedback.
